assertions.text: Both vehicles (V1 and V2) were traveling northbound on a four-lane, physically divided, bidirectional roadway with two northbound lanes and two southbound lanes.
Both vehicles were negotiating a right-hand curve in the roadway.
The roadway had a slight incline where the vehicles were traveling.
V1 was traveling in lane one (the leftmost northbound lane) and V2 was traveling in lane two (the rightmost northbound lane) at the time described.
V1 began to move from lane one into lane two while V2 was attempting to pass V1.
V2 departed its lane to the right prior to impact.
V2's frontal plane impacted V1's rear plane (V2 struck the rear of V1 with its front).
There was a single impact between V2 and V1 described (frontal of V2 to rear of V1).
After impact, V2 came to final rest off the right side of the roadway.
There was a small fire in the engine compartment of V2 after the collision.
V1 continued northbound after the collision and was not located at the scene.

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry provides vehicle speed and EDR summary indicating two vehicles in crash (["vehicles_in_crash":2](T1)) and V2 was moving northbound is not directly in sensors; road layout (four-lane, physically divided, bidirectional with two northbound/southbound lanes) is not recorded by vehicle sensors, so this is unverifiable.

Steering shows a rightward turn prior to impact with steering angle moving from negative to positive, peaking at [30.0 deg](T2) at t=0.0; however telemetry cannot confirm roadway curvature or that both vehicles were negotiating a right-hand curve, so this is unverifiable.
No slope/incline sensors are available in the telemetry; the available channels do not record road grade, so the claim of a slight incline is unverifiable.
Lane positions (V1 in lane one, V2 in lane two) are not recorded by the vehicle telemetry; steering and speed do not provide lane identifiers, so this is unverifiable.
There is no data from V1 and no lane-change detection from V2 sensors to confirm the sequence 'V1 moved into V2's lane while V2 was passing', so this sequence is unverifiable.

Lateral delta-V cumulative peak is [11.0 km/h](T4) during impact at [t=0.09 s](T4), indicating V2 experienced lateral motion consistent with departing its lane to the right prior to impact; steering also moves right to [30.0 deg](T2) at impact and brake applied at [t=-0.5 s](T4). These telemetry features support that V2 departed its lane to the right prior to impact.

EDR summary labels the event as 'Frontal / Rear Crash (Record 1)' in the window info (["event_desc":"Frontal / Rear Crash (Record 1)"])(T1) but the delta-v longitudinal peak is [81.0 km/h](T4) negative signed indicating a large rearward delta-V on V2 (V2 experienced a longitudinal delta-V of [ -81.0 km/h](T4)), which is inconsistent with V2's front striking V1's rear; the telemetry indicates V2 experienced a large negative longitudinal delta-V consistent with being struck from behind rather than delivering a frontal impact.

The telemetry records a single crash pulse with peaks in longitudinal ([81.0 km/h](T4)) and lateral ([11.0 km/h](T4)) delta-V, but it cannot alone determine the count of distinct impacts between vehicles; number of impacts (single vs multiple contacts) is therefore unverifiable.
Final rest position off the right side of the roadway is not recorded by the vehicle telemetry (no GPS position series is provided), so this is unverifiable.
Fire in the engine compartment is not a telemetry channel; no diagnostic or fire-detection data present, so presence of fire is unverifiable.
V1 continuing northbound and not being located at scene is not something vehicle V2 sensors can confirm; unverifiable.

result.rationale: Telemetry from V2 provides strong evidence about vehicle kinematics during the crash pulse (notably a large longitudinal delta-V of [81.0 km/h](T4) and lateral delta-V of [11.0 km/h](T4), steering peak [30.0 deg](T2), and a brake application at [t=-0.5 s](T4)), but lacks positional lane-level, road geometry, fire, and other-vehicle motion data required to verify most narrative assertions.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept

result.error: compute_stats() got an unexpected keyword argument 'max_points_per_channel'

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

accel_pedal.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
